content:
Hello! I'm new to Nova and to Linux contribution as a whole. I am looking for some help with navigating and starting my first contribution. I looked through the Task list linked from the Nova GPU Driver but Dave Airlie mentioned that some/most of them are done.

Are there any tasks that would be good for someone new? I have experience with mostly no_std rust through my embedded work so I'm hoping that background carries over well.

You can also check the R4L good first issues in general, a lot of the stuff there will benefit Nova eventually. Right now indeed I think most of the things in our task list have someone active on them

Is there anything that you can think of that I could work on in Nova? Unless there's nothing good for someone new of course. I'm really interested in Nova and it's partially the reason I upgraded my old pascal card to a Blackwell one.

Well thinking again about it, we need someone to fix #Nova > `Cmdq::new` uses excessive stack size . It can actually be a good first task if you are familiar with Rust, and it doesn't require any GPU-specific knowledge
Note that the fix might be as inelegant as dismantling PteArray and just writing the page tables one by one :)

You know, funny enough, I had a similar issue with excessive stack usage in one of my embedded rust projects when both wifi and bluetooth were involved.

In any case, after reading the thread, it sounds interesting! I'd love to work on that :)

I imagine I don't need more information apart from what written already? And I have questions, I should send a message on that thread basically?
yes, I think this thread has all the information needed to implement a fix. And of course, feel free to ask questions or discuss potential solutions there!
